description: A young, single-stemmed sapling with large pinnately compound leaves. It emits a pungent, spicy-citrus odor when bruised.
A slender young tree with smooth, gray-brown bark and a few sturdy branches bearing long compound leaves.
A towering canopy tree with dark, deeply furrowed bark. Its large compound leaves form a high, dappled canopy.
A towering canopy tree. Round, bright green fruits hang heavily among the large compound leaves.
The tree's leaves turn yellow and begin to drop. Large, yellowish-green to blackish fruits are visible on the branches and the ground.
A massive, bare silhouette. Its dark, deeply furrowed bark forms interlacing diamond patterns on the thick trunk.
Long compound leaves with many small leaflets. Strong spicy-citrus scent when crushed.
Yellow, drying compound leaves preparing to drop from the tree.
Stout, dark brown branches bearing prominent leaf scars.
Thick, deeply furrowed dark bark forming an interlacing diamond pattern.
A thick, deep taproot branching into dark, heavy lateral roots.
A large, heavy, bright green sphere. The thick fleshy husk smells sharply of citrus and spice.
A dark brown to black, mushy or dried spherical husk. It smells pungent and earthy.
A very hard, heavily ridged, dark brown nut shell.
Rich, oily, intricately folded nut meat. Darker than most nuts.
Chunks of thick, fibrous green or black husk. Stains everything it touches a dark brown.
Thick, woody, deeply ridged shell fragments.
